subject: Geography
title: The Colorado River
description: A fast breakdown of The Colorado River for builders.

hook.text: The Colorado River carved the Grand Canyon over six million years.

buildup.text: It provides water to 40 million people across seven US states and Mexico.

discovery.text: The Hoover Dam tamed the river in 1936, creating Lake Mead.

twist.text: So much water is diverted that the river often dries up before reaching the sea.

climax.text: Lake Mead has dropped to record lows, exposing a 'bathtub ring' on canyon walls.

punchline.text: A canyon-carving river now vanishes in the desert.

quiz.question: What natural landmark did the Colorado River carve?
